questions.promptHtml: A bank is piloting an AI mortgage assistant. Regulators require the bank to prove applicants in similar financial situations receive consistent outcomes across protected groups. Which responsible AI principle is being validated?
questions.correctLabel: Fairness. Fairness focuses on preventing disproportionate impact across similarly situated groups and reducing discriminatory outcomes.
questions.extraHtml: Transparency explains decisions, while accountability defines who owns remediation. Fairness is the principle directly tied to parity checks and bias mitigation in outcomes.

questions.promptHtml: An insurer must reject unsafe model outputs before they reach customers in a claim-support chatbot. The team needs pre-response safety enforcement on generated text. Which control is most appropriate?
questions.correctLabel: Add content filtering and moderation. Safety filters are designed to detect and block harmful output categories before delivery.
questions.extraHtml: Max tokens changes length, and temperature changes randomness. Neither is a safety policy control. Filters enforce policy boundaries.

questions.promptHtml: A public-sector AI service must continue safe operation during transient model failures and uncertain predictions. Which responsible AI consideration is most directly addressed?
questions.correctLabel: Reliability and safety. This principle covers robust performance, graceful failure handling, and avoidance of unsafe behavior in edge cases.
questions.extraHtml: Inclusiveness is about accessible design across user abilities. Transparency is about explainability. Reliability/safety targets dependable behavior under stress.

questions.promptHtml: A healthcare startup must protect PHI used by an AI triage model and restrict operator access to least privilege. Which principle is primary?
questions.correctLabel: Privacy and security. It requires strong data protection, access controls, and secure handling of sensitive information.
questions.extraHtml: Accountability assigns ownership; fairness addresses bias. The requirement is confidentiality and secure access governance.

questions.promptHtml: A transit authority deploys a voice bot for riders with hearing and speech variability, including assistive workflows. Which principle is best reflected?
questions.correctLabel: Inclusiveness. It emphasizes designing AI systems usable by people with diverse needs and abilities.
questions.extraHtml: Reliability is operational stability; transparency is disclosure. Inclusive design ensures broader accessibility and equitable usability.

questions.promptHtml: A customer support bot must clearly disclose that users are interacting with AI and provide confidence/limitations. Which principle is this?
questions.correctLabel: Transparency. It requires clear communication about AI usage, behavior, and limitations.
questions.extraHtml: Accountability defines ownership after harm; privacy secures data. Disclosure and explainability are transparency objectives.

questions.promptHtml: A company creates governance playbooks defining who approves model releases and who handles incident response for AI harm. Which principle is central?
questions.correctLabel: Accountability. It establishes human responsibility for AI decisions, oversight, and remediation actions.
questions.extraHtml: Fairness and inclusiveness guide outcomes and design, but accountability ensures there is an owner for decisions and corrective action.

questions.promptHtml: A retailer wants to auto-tag product images, detect shelf stockouts from photos, and transcribe scanned labels. Which statement correctly maps workloads?

options.label: Vision for image tasks, OCR for text in images, NLP for text understanding
NLP for image tagging, Speech for OCR, Vision for sentiment
Speech for object detection, NLP for facial detection, Vision for translation

questions.correctLabel: Vision for image tasks, OCR for text in images, NLP for text understanding. This matches common AI workload boundaries.
questions.extraHtml: Option B and C mix service categories incorrectly. Correct architectural mapping reduces design errors and cost.

questions.promptHtml: A legal firm needs to extract clauses and signatures from scanned contracts and also summarize obligations in plain language. Which pair best matches?

options.label: Document Intelligence + Azure AI Language/Azure OpenAI

questions.correctLabel: Document Intelligence + Azure AI Language/Azure OpenAI. Document extraction and language summarization are separate but complementary workloads.
questions.extraHtml: Face and Translator do not parse contract structure. Speech/Anomaly Detector are unrelated to contract extraction and summarization.

questions.promptHtml: A support center wants an AI system to classify ticket urgency and route to teams based on historical labels. Which workload is primarily involved?
questions.correctLabel: Supervised machine learning classification. Labeled historical outcomes imply supervised category prediction.
questions.extraHtml: Clustering lacks predefined labels. Object detection is computer vision for spatial localization, not ticket routing.

questions.promptHtml: A media company needs subtitles in near real time from live audio streams and then sentiment trend analysis by minute. Which workload pair is correct?
questions.correctLabel: Speech-to-text then NLP sentiment analysis. Audio must first be transcribed, then text can be analyzed for sentiment.
questions.extraHtml: OCR is for text in images, not audio. CLU handles intent/entity extraction, not minute-by-minute sentiment pipelines.

questions.promptHtml: A security team wants a model that generates incident summaries and response drafts, but also provides citations from internal runbooks. Which AI pattern is this?
questions.correctLabel: Retrieval-augmented generation workload. It combines generation with retrieval from trusted documents to produce grounded responses.
questions.extraHtml: Classification and clustering do not generate contextual narratives with source-grounded evidence.

questions.promptHtml: A startup needs to detect unusual transaction spikes without labeled fraud examples. Which machine learning approach is most suitable initially?
questions.correctLabel: Unsupervised clustering/anomaly-style pattern discovery. Unlabeled data calls for pattern discovery approaches first.
questions.extraHtml: Classification needs labeled fraud outcomes. Regression predicts continuous values, not latent pattern groups.

questions.promptHtml: An operations model predicts hourly energy demand in kWh for 30 days ahead. Which task type is this?
questions.correctLabel: Time-series forecasting (regression family). The target is a future continuous numeric value indexed by time.
questions.extraHtml: Binary classification predicts labels; clustering groups records. Forecasting handles temporal sequence and numeric outputs.

questions.promptHtml: A fraud model outputs fraud/not-fraud. The team wants to reduce false positives that block valid purchases. Which metric should they emphasize?
questions.correctLabel: Precision. Precision improves when fewer predicted fraud cases are actually legitimate, reducing false positives.
questions.extraHtml: Recall targets finding more actual fraud (fewer false negatives). MAE is a regression metric, not classification quality for this scenario.

questions.promptHtml: A cancer-screening classifier must miss as few true cancer cases as possible. Which metric should be prioritized?
questions.correctLabel: Recall. Recall maximizes detection of actual positive cases, minimizing false negatives.
questions.extraHtml: Precision reduces false positives but can still miss positives. R-squared applies to regression, not binary screening.

questions.promptHtml: A model has 99% accuracy on training data but degrades sharply on validation data. What is the most likely issue?
questions.correctLabel: Overfitting. The model memorizes training patterns/noise and fails to generalize to unseen data.
questions.extraHtml: Underfitting performs poorly on both train and validation sets. Encryption is unrelated to model generalization behavior.

questions.promptHtml: A team splits data into train, validation, and test sets. What is the best role of validation data?

options.label: Final unbiased metric only after all tuning

questions.correctLabel: Hyperparameter/model selection before final test. Validation guides model choices while preserving test data for final evaluation.
questions.extraHtml: The final test set should remain untouched during tuning. Archive storage is not the purpose of dataset splits.

questions.promptHtml: A project dataset has input columns such as income and loan term, and target column approved/denied. Which statement is correct?

options.label: Income and loan term are features; approved/denied is label
Approved/denied is a feature; income is a label

questions.correctLabel: Income and loan term are features; approved/denied is label. Features are inputs, labels are outcomes to predict.
questions.extraHtml: Swapping label/feature breaks supervised learning setup and invalidates model training objectives.

questions.promptHtml: A startup wants no-code/low-code experiments that automatically try many algorithms and hyperparameters for tabular data. Which Azure capability fits?

options.label: Automated ML in Azure Machine Learning

questions.correctLabel: Automated ML in Azure Machine Learning. AutoML orchestrates model/featurization/hyperparameter search for supported tasks.
questions.extraHtml: Face and Translator are prebuilt domain APIs, not general tabular model search frameworks.

questions.promptHtml: A team needs scalable training that can start at zero nodes and scale out only while jobs run. Which Azure ML compute option is best?
questions.correctLabel: Compute cluster. It supports queued jobs and autoscaling for cost-efficient training workloads.
questions.extraHtml: Compute instances are personal dev machines, not elastic multi-job clusters. Local-only lacks managed scaling and orchestration.

questions.promptHtml: A team must expose a trained model with managed auth, scaling, and traffic splitting between versions. Which capability addresses this?
questions.correctLabel: Azure ML managed online endpoints. They provide API hosting, scaling, auth, and canary/A-B routing for model versions.
questions.extraHtml: Blob lifecycle policies manage storage tiers. Vision Analyze Image is a prebuilt inference API, not custom model deployment orchestration.

questions.promptHtml: A model predicts continuous values, but stakeholders request category predictions (low, medium, high risk). Which task transition is required?
questions.correctLabel: Move from regression to classification. The output changed from numeric continuous to discrete categories.
questions.extraHtml: Clustering is unlabeled grouping and does not directly map to predefined risk bands unless labels are defined.

questions.promptHtml: A team compares two model versions in production by sending 90% traffic to v1 and 10% to v2, then evaluates business KPIs. What practice is this?
questions.correctLabel: A/B testing (champion/challenger). Controlled traffic splitting validates new versions with lower rollout risk.
questions.extraHtml: Feature scaling and labeling happen before deployment; they do not provide runtime comparative performance evidence.

questions.promptHtml: A multilingual support model degrades after adding many noisy low-quality labels. Which statement best explains the impact?

options.label: Data quality heavily drives model quality
VM SKU alone determines model quality

questions.correctLabel: Data quality heavily drives model quality. Poor labels introduce noise and degrade learnable signal.
questions.extraHtml: Compute can speed training but cannot fix fundamentally wrong supervision. Interface visuals have no training relevance.

questions.promptHtml: A model training run is slow. The team asks if moving to a larger GPU guarantees higher predictive accuracy. What is correct?

options.label: Larger compute mainly affects training speed/capacity, not guaranteed accuracy
Compute choice is irrelevant to training time

questions.correctLabel: Larger compute mainly affects training speed/capacity, not guaranteed accuracy. Accuracy depends on data, objective, and model design.
questions.extraHtml: B is a common misconception. C is false because hardware and parallelism strongly influence runtime.

questions.promptHtml: A team wants explainability for why a tabular model predicts loan denial for a user. Which Azure ML capability is most aligned?

options.label: Model explainability tools in Azure ML/Responsible AI dashboard

questions.correctLabel: Model explainability tools in Azure ML/Responsible AI dashboard. These tools help interpret feature contributions and model behavior.
questions.extraHtml: OCR and Face APIs are task-specific cognitive services and do not explain tabular model decision features.

questions.promptHtml: A photo app must assign one overall label to each image (forest, beach, city). Which vision task is this?
questions.correctLabel: Image classification. It predicts a class for the entire image rather than locating many items.
questions.extraHtml: Object detection returns bounding boxes for multiple objects. OCR extracts text, not scene categories.

questions.promptHtml: A warehouse camera solution must locate and count pallets in each frame with coordinates for robotic picking. Which task fits?
questions.correctLabel: Object detection. It identifies object classes and spatial locations via bounding boxes.
questions.extraHtml: Classification cannot provide positions. Sentiment is NLP for text emotions, unrelated to image localization.

questions.promptHtml: An invoice processing system needs to read printed totals and line-item text from scanned pages. Which capability is required?
questions.correctLabel: OCR/Read. OCR extracts machine-readable text from image or document pixels.
questions.extraHtml: Face detection identifies facial regions, not invoice text fields. Speech synthesis converts text to audio.

questions.promptHtml: A retail kiosk must detect whether a face is present and estimate attributes like head pose for framing quality. Which service is most specific?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Face. It specializes in face detection and face-related analysis capabilities.
questions.extraHtml: Translator handles language conversion, and AI Search indexes content. Neither provides specialized facial analysis.

questions.promptHtml: A manufacturer needs broad image tagging, captioning, and visual feature extraction without training a custom model. Which service should be first choice?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Vision. It offers prebuilt image analysis capabilities for common vision tasks.
questions.extraHtml: AutoML tabular is not a drop-in image API. Speech services process audio, not image semantics.

questions.promptHtml: A team needs a no-code way to train a custom detector for company-specific logos on product photos. Which service is most suitable?
questions.correctLabel: Azure Custom Vision. It supports custom image classification and object detection with labeled examples.
questions.extraHtml: Translator and Speech do not train custom image detectors. Custom Vision is purpose-built for this scenario.

questions.promptHtml: A compliance team needs extraction of key-value pairs and tables from forms while preserving document structure. Which Azure service is best?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Document Intelligence. It combines OCR with layout/field extraction for structured document understanding.
questions.extraHtml: Face and key phrase extraction do not provide robust form table/key-value parsing from scanned documents.

questions.promptHtml: A museum app translates text from exhibit photos into multiple languages. What pipeline is correct?
questions.correctLabel: OCR then translation. Text must be extracted first before language translation can occur.
questions.extraHtml: Object and face detection do not provide textual content. Speech recognition is for audio, not printed signs.

questions.promptHtml: A media archive needs transcripts and scene-level indexing for thousands of videos, including searchable moments. Which service is best aligned?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Video Indexer. It provides rich timeline insights like transcription, scenes, and searchable metadata.
questions.extraHtml: Face-only analysis misses broader audiovisual indexing. SQL stores structured data but does not generate video AI insights.

questions.promptHtml: A highway camera model must flag multiple classes (car, truck, bus) and also return each object position for counting lanes. Which output is required?
questions.correctLabel: Class labels plus bounding boxes. This is the defining output format of object detection.
questions.extraHtml: Single-label classification cannot support per-object lane counts. Sentiment applies to text, not traffic objects.

questions.promptHtml: A banking app uses OCR on check images and receives low confidence in handwritten fields. What is the best interpretation?

options.label: Confidence scores indicate extraction certainty and should drive human review thresholds
Confidence scores are unrelated to OCR quality

questions.correctLabel: Confidence scores indicate extraction certainty and should drive human review thresholds. This supports safer human-in-the-loop handling.
questions.extraHtml: Ignoring confidence raises error risk. Translation failure is separate from OCR uncertainty on handwriting.

questions.promptHtml: A smartphone app must run image classification offline on-device for latency/privacy reasons. Which approach is most aligned?

options.label: Train/deploy exportable custom vision model for edge inference
Use SQL queries for image inference

questions.correctLabel: Train/deploy exportable custom vision model for edge inference. Custom models can be exported to supported formats for local use.
questions.extraHtml: Speech and SQL do not provide local image inference. Edge deployment is key for offline and low-latency requirements.

questions.promptHtml: A security operations team needs to blur faces in stored surveillance snapshots before analyst review. Which capability directly enables face localization?
questions.correctLabel: Face detection coordinates. Bounding regions allow deterministic redaction/blurring workflows.
questions.extraHtml: NLP and speech features do not return pixel coordinates for redaction in images.

questions.promptHtml: An accessibility product needs automatic alt-text generation for uploaded images. Which service is most appropriate to start?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Vision image captioning/analysis. It can generate descriptions and tags from visual content.
questions.extraHtml: Speech translation handles audio. Event Hubs ingests streams but does not generate image captions.

questions.promptHtml: A legal team needs language detection before routing documents to the correct regional NLP pipeline. Which capability is needed first?
questions.correctLabel: Language detection. Detecting language enables selection of the appropriate downstream model/process.
questions.extraHtml: Sentiment can run later; object detection is a vision task and unrelated to text-language routing.

questions.promptHtml: A support platform must identify customer intent like ResetPassword and extract entities such as account type and region. Which capability is best?
questions.correctLabel: Conversational Language Understanding (CLU). CLU is designed for intent recognition and entity extraction in conversational text.
questions.extraHtml: OCR/image classification are vision tasks. CLU is the NLP choice for intent/entity-driven conversational apps.

questions.promptHtml: A compliance system needs to find all person names, organizations, locations, and dates across contracts. Which NLP feature applies?
questions.correctLabel: Named Entity Recognition (NER). NER extracts and categorizes entities from text spans.
questions.extraHtml: Summarization condenses text but does not necessarily classify entities. Speech synthesis converts text to audio.

questions.promptHtml: A product team wants quick extraction of the main concepts from long customer feedback, not full summaries. Which feature is most direct?
questions.correctLabel: Key phrase extraction. It identifies salient terms/phrases representing document themes.
questions.extraHtml: Translation changes language; face detection is unrelated. Key phrase extraction is lightweight topic surfacing.

questions.promptHtml: A retailer wants each review sentence scored as positive/neutral/negative and flagged for mixed tone. Which capability fits?
questions.correctLabel: Sentiment analysis with opinion mining. It provides polarity and confidence at document/sentence granularity.
questions.extraHtml: Language detection does not measure sentiment. OCR is for images/documents, not sentiment of textual meaning.

questions.promptHtml: A global help desk must translate inbound text tickets into English and preserve formatting for attached docs. Which service is most suitable?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Translator (text/document translation). It supports multilingual text translation and document translation scenarios.
questions.extraHtml: Face and Search are not translation engines. Translator is purpose-built for linguistic conversion.

questions.promptHtml: A call center wants live transcription of agent calls and synthesized spoken responses from text templates. Which capability pair is required?
questions.correctLabel: Speech-to-text and text-to-speech. This combines audio transcription with synthesized playback.
questions.extraHtml: OCR/key phrases operate on text or images, not end-to-end speech audio interaction.

questions.promptHtml: A virtual assistant must classify user utterances into intents with confidence scores and fallback when confidence is low. Which Azure service is core?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Language (CLU). It returns intents/entities with confidence for routing and fallback logic.
questions.extraHtml: Vision is irrelevant to text intents. Content Safety screens harm, not domain intent classification.

questions.promptHtml: A voice bot must understand medical vocabulary and accents common in one region. Which enhancement improves recognition quality?
questions.correctLabel: Custom Speech. It adapts recognition with domain/audio data for improved specialized transcription accuracy.
questions.extraHtml: Dictionary lookup alone does not adapt acoustic/language models comprehensively. Face is unrelated to speech recognition.

questions.promptHtml: A multilingual chatbot first detects language and then routes to locale-specific CLU projects. Why is this pattern useful?

options.label: It improves intent accuracy by applying language-appropriate models
It eliminates the need for any training examples
It converts NLP into computer vision

questions.correctLabel: It improves intent accuracy by applying language-appropriate models. Locale-specific models typically perform better than one-size-fits-all routing.
questions.extraHtml: You still need training data. The pattern stays within NLP and does not convert modalities.

questions.promptHtml: A finance team wants to detect references to account numbers and addresses in text before storage. Which NLP capability is most directly relevant?

options.label: Entity extraction (including PII-focused analysis where applicable)

questions.correctLabel: Entity extraction (including PII-focused analysis where applicable). It identifies sensitive text spans for masking/governance workflows.
questions.extraHtml: Speech synthesis and object detection cannot locate sensitive entities in text documents.

questions.promptHtml: An application needs abstractive summaries of long policy documents for managers. Which capability family applies?

options.label: Language service summarization / generative summarization patterns

questions.correctLabel: Language service summarization / generative summarization patterns. Summarization condenses long text into concise output.
questions.extraHtml: Face detection is visual. OCR only extracts text and does not produce semantic summaries.

questions.promptHtml: A QA team sees sentiment drift between product lines. They need aspect-level polarity (battery positive, support negative) in the same review. Which feature is best?
questions.correctLabel: Opinion mining with sentiment analysis. It links sentiment to targets/aspects inside text.
questions.extraHtml: Language detection and translation do not identify per-aspect sentiment within a single review.

questions.promptHtml: A voice app for warehouses needs punctuation and speaker segmentation in meeting transcripts. Which feature set should be enabled?

options.label: Azure AI Speech transcription with diarization and formatting

questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Speech transcription with diarization and formatting. It supports speaker-aware transcript generation.
questions.extraHtml: Key phrase extraction works after transcription, and OCR layout is for documents/images, not spoken meetings.

questions.promptHtml: A startup wants a model that can create new text and code based on prompts rather than only classify input. Which model type is this?
questions.correctLabel: Generative model. Generative models produce novel content by modeling data distributions and context.
questions.extraHtml: Discriminative models are optimized for prediction/classification; clustering groups data and does not generate fluent code/text.

questions.promptHtml: A team asks why transformer-based LLMs outperform older sequence models on context handling. Which explanation is strongest?

options.label: Self-attention captures long-range token relationships efficiently
They only process one token per request

questions.correctLabel: Self-attention captures long-range token relationships efficiently. This is a core advantage of transformer architectures.
questions.extraHtml: Transformers do not ignore context; they are built for contextual dependency modeling at scale.

questions.promptHtml: A legal assistant must answer only from approved policy documents and cite sources to reduce hallucinations. Which pattern should be used?
questions.correctLabel: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). RAG grounds generation in retrieved enterprise documents and enables citation workflows.
questions.extraHtml: Higher temperature increases variability, often worsening factual consistency. Removing system guidance weakens governance.

questions.promptHtml: A team chooses Azure OpenAI instead of public endpoints because they need enterprise controls such as private networking and compliance posture. Which choice best fits?
questions.correctLabel: Azure OpenAI Service. It provides Azure-integrated security, governance, and enterprise deployment controls around OpenAI models.
questions.extraHtml: Functions and Monitor are supporting services, not managed access to GPT-class foundation models.

questions.promptHtml: A product owner wants to compare multiple foundation models from different providers before deployment in one place. Which capability is designed for this?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Foundry model catalog. It centralizes discoverability/evaluation/deployment options for foundation models.
questions.extraHtml: DNS and lifecycle policies manage networking/storage, not model marketplace and evaluation workflows.

questions.promptHtml: A team needs a visual way to orchestrate prompts, model calls, and Python nodes with traceability. Which Foundry feature is best?
questions.correctLabel: Prompt flow. It supports multi-step LLM workflows with testing and observability for AI app development.
questions.extraHtml: Bastion/Backup are infrastructure services, not LLM workflow orchestration tooling.

questions.promptHtml: A team tunes temperature from 0.2 to 0.9 and sees more diverse but less deterministic responses. What parameter behavior does this confirm?
questions.correctLabel: Temperature controls randomness/creativity tradeoff. Higher values sample more diverse token choices.
questions.extraHtml: Authentication and index sizing are unrelated to token sampling behavior during generation.

questions.promptHtml: A support bot must never expose secret keys if users attempt prompt injection. Which control is most direct in app architecture?

options.label: Keep secrets server-side and apply strict tool/function allowlists

questions.correctLabel: Keep secrets server-side and apply strict tool/function allowlists. Security boundaries must be enforced outside model text generation.
questions.extraHtml: Token limits and UI styling do not enforce secret-protection controls against malicious prompts.

questions.promptHtml: A team wants to reduce irrelevant long answers and force strict JSON schema output for downstream systems. Which approach is best?

options.label: Prompt engineering with explicit format constraints and examples

questions.correctLabel: Prompt engineering with explicit format constraints and examples. Clear output contracts significantly improve structure adherence.
questions.extraHtml: High temperature increases variation. Removing system instructions weakens consistency and policy alignment.

questions.promptHtml: A finance chatbot provides outdated policy advice because model pretraining is old. Which pattern best keeps responses current without full retraining?
questions.correctLabel: RAG over fresh indexed content. Retrieval from current documents injects up-to-date facts at inference time.
questions.extraHtml: Token limits affect length, not freshness. OCR extracts text but does not add retrieval and grounding logic by itself.

questions.promptHtml: A company wants to test prompt changes safely before broad release and compare groundedness/harm metrics. Which workflow is best?

options.label: Evaluate in controlled environment and deploy via staged rollout
Replace production prompt immediately for all users

questions.correctLabel: Evaluate in controlled environment and deploy via staged rollout. This minimizes risk and supports measurable quality gates.
questions.extraHtml: Immediate full rollout raises blast radius. Disabling filters undermines responsible AI requirements.

questions.promptHtml: A team needs embeddings to power semantic search over internal docs. Which model capability is required?
questions.correctLabel: Text embedding model. Embeddings map text into vectors for similarity search and retrieval pipelines.
questions.extraHtml: Captioning/synthesis do not generate semantic vector representations for vector search indexing.

questions.promptHtml: A model generates unsafe responses for some prompts. Which built-in Azure OpenAI mechanism should be checked first?

options.label: Content filters and configured severity thresholds

questions.correctLabel: Content filters and configured severity thresholds. These controls govern harmful prompt/output detection policies.
questions.extraHtml: Compute and CDN settings do not classify harmful content categories in model interactions.

questions.promptHtml: A CTO asks whether fine-tuning is always required to build a useful enterprise chatbot. Which answer is best?

options.label: No; prompt engineering plus RAG often works without fine-tuning
Yes; every chatbot must be fine-tuned first
No; because LLMs need no governance

questions.correctLabel: No; prompt engineering plus RAG often works without fine-tuning. Many use cases can be delivered by grounding and orchestration first.
questions.extraHtml: B overstates requirements and cost. C is incorrect because governance remains mandatory regardless of tuning strategy.

questions.promptHtml: A support assistant should escalate uncertain answers to a human agent. Which design is most appropriate?
questions.correctLabel: Confidence/grounding checks with fallback routing. Human-in-the-loop reduces risk when confidence is low or context is weak.
questions.extraHtml: Disabling history can reduce context. High creativity can increase hallucination risk in support scenarios.

questions.promptHtml: A team wants to expose company APIs to a chat model for deterministic actions like ticket creation. Which capability pattern is relevant?
questions.correctLabel: Tool/function calling with constrained schemas. It lets the model select predefined operations while systems execute safely.
questions.extraHtml: OCR and diarization are unrelated modalities and do not provide transactional API orchestration.

questions.promptHtml: A team evaluates two LLM prompts: one verbose, one concise with explicit role/constraints/examples. Which usually gives more reliable output?

options.label: The concise but explicit structured prompt
The longest prompt regardless of clarity
A prompt with no task instructions

questions.correctLabel: The concise but explicit structured prompt. Clarity, constraints, and examples matter more than raw length.
questions.extraHtml: Long prompts can add noise and token cost. Missing instructions increases ambiguity and unstable outputs.

questions.promptHtml: A company needs to block copyrighted text reproduction in generated outputs where possible. Which safety capability is relevant?

options.label: Protected material detection and policy controls

questions.correctLabel: Protected material detection and policy controls. These controls help identify and govern risky copyrighted output patterns.
questions.extraHtml: Sampling parameters do not enforce copyright checks. OCR is not a copyright-output safeguard by itself.

questions.promptHtml: A product team must compare model choices for cost, latency, and quality on the same task before production. Which practice is best?

options.label: Structured model benchmarking/evaluation across candidate models
Choose the largest model by default

questions.correctLabel: Structured model benchmarking/evaluation across candidate models. Tradeoff-based evaluation prevents overpaying for unnecessary model capacity.
questions.extraHtml: Largest models can be expensive and slower. Ignoring non-quality metrics leads to impractical production architectures.

questions.promptHtml: A developer asks when to use embeddings plus search versus direct chat completion only. Which statement is most accurate?

options.label: Use embeddings/search when retrieval over external knowledge is required
Use direct chat only for all enterprise knowledge scenarios
Use embeddings only for image generation

questions.correctLabel: Use embeddings/search when retrieval over external knowledge is required. Vector retrieval is central to grounding external corpora.
questions.extraHtml: Direct chat without retrieval often hallucinates or misses proprietary context. Embeddings are not limited to image generation.

questions.promptHtml: A customer support bot must maintain conversational context across turns while respecting token limits. Which approach is best?

options.label: Keep rolling conversation history with summarization/windowing
Send only last user turn every time

questions.correctLabel: Keep rolling conversation history with summarization/windowing. This balances context retention with context-window constraints.
questions.extraHtml: Single-turn requests lose continuity. Retraining per user session is infeasible and unnecessary.

questions.promptHtml: A compliance officer asks how to reduce jailbreak risk in a public chatbot. Which mitigation is strongest?

options.label: Defense-in-depth with system instructions, input filtering, and tool restrictions
Increase temperature to make answers varied

questions.correctLabel: Defense-in-depth with system instructions, input filtering, and tool restrictions. Multi-layer controls are required against prompt attacks.
questions.extraHtml: Temperature is not a jailbreak defense. Disabling logs reduces forensic visibility and governance quality.

questions.promptHtml: A startup needs to generate marketing images from textual prompts within Azure governance boundaries. Which service is most appropriate?
questions.correctLabel: Azure OpenAI image generation models. They support prompt-based image creation under Azure controls.
questions.extraHtml: SQL and speech synthesis do not generate images from prompts.

questions.promptHtml: A team needs to orchestrate retrieval, prompt templating, and model response scoring in one reproducible workflow. Which Foundry capability best matches?
questions.correctLabel: Prompt flow with evaluation steps. It supports modular LLM pipelines and repeatable test/evaluation runs.
questions.extraHtml: Backup and Front Door are infrastructure services, not AI workflow orchestration and scoring frameworks.

questions.promptHtml: A legal-genAI app must provide traceable citations for each answer sentence. Which architectural choice is most aligned?

options.label: RAG with source-passage return and citation formatting
Pure free-form generation with no retrieval

questions.correctLabel: RAG with source-passage return and citation formatting. Retrieval provenance enables traceability and auditability.
questions.extraHtml: Free-form generation lacks provenance guarantees. OCR alone does not perform semantic retrieval and citation linking.

questions.promptHtml: A platform team needs one exam-like distinction: Azure AI Foundry vs Azure OpenAI Service. Which is most accurate?

options.label: Foundry is an app-development environment; Azure OpenAI is model API service access
Foundry is only storage; OpenAI is only networking

questions.correctLabel: Foundry is an app-development environment; Azure OpenAI is model API service access. They are complementary, not identical.
questions.extraHtml: Foundry spans broader lifecycle tooling. Azure OpenAI specifically exposes OpenAI models within Azure controls.

questions.promptHtml: A company wants to route user prompts through policy checks before model execution and block high-risk categories. Which step is essential?
questions.correctLabel: Pre-inference input moderation pipeline. Screening prompts before inference helps prevent disallowed request handling.
questions.extraHtml: Context window size does not enforce policy. Removing role messages weakens guardrails.

questions.promptHtml: A question-answering app must reduce repetitive outputs while preserving factuality. Which parameter can help repetition control?
questions.correctLabel: Frequency penalty. It can discourage repeated tokens/phrases during generation.
questions.extraHtml: Disk size and OCR confidence are unrelated to token-level repetition behavior in text generation.

questions.promptHtml: A retailer wants to enrich product listings by extracting keywords from descriptions, tagging images, and transcribing customer call snippets. Which workload mapping is correct?

options.label: NLP for text keywords, vision for images, speech-to-text for calls
Speech for image tagging and OCR

questions.correctLabel: NLP for text keywords, vision for images, speech-to-text for calls. Each task maps to its native AI modality.
questions.extraHtml: Using one modality for all tasks is a common design mistake. Correct workload mapping improves accuracy and cost efficiency.

questions.promptHtml: A hospital chatbot must mask personal identifiers before storing conversation logs and enforce least-privilege data access. Which principle is most directly addressed?
questions.correctLabel: Privacy and security. It focuses on protecting sensitive data and controlling access throughout the AI lifecycle.
questions.extraHtml: Transparency explains behavior, and inclusiveness improves accessibility. Data protection controls map to privacy and security.

questions.promptHtml: A lender uses an AI score for pre-approval and must document who approves model updates and who owns customer remediation actions. Which principle is this?
questions.correctLabel: Accountability. It defines ownership for model governance, operational decisions, and corrective actions.
questions.extraHtml: Fairness governs outcomes across groups, and reliability covers stable behavior. Governance ownership is accountability.

questions.promptHtml: A municipal service app must support voice and text interactions for users with diverse accessibility needs and varied interaction styles. Which principle is best aligned?
questions.correctLabel: Inclusiveness. It emphasizes designing AI systems that are usable by people with a wide range of abilities and contexts.
questions.extraHtml: Privacy/security protects data, while accountability addresses governance ownership. Inclusiveness is about broad accessibility by design.

questions.promptHtml: A legal discovery solution must parse scanned contracts into searchable entities and then support semantic retrieval over extracted text. Which architecture is best?

options.label: Document extraction pipeline plus Azure AI Search indexing

questions.correctLabel: Document extraction pipeline plus Azure AI Search indexing. This combines document understanding with retrieval for knowledge mining.
questions.extraHtml: Face and speech services do not provide contract-centric retrieval architecture. Search indexing is required for semantic discovery workflows.

questions.promptHtml: A public agency requires all citizens to know when a decision is AI-assisted and to view key factors behind decisions. Which principle is central?
questions.correctLabel: Transparency. It requires communicating AI use and making system behavior understandable to stakeholders.
questions.extraHtml: Fairness addresses equitable outcomes and inclusiveness addresses usability. Disclosure and explainability are transparency goals.

questions.promptHtml: A model performs unusually well in validation because duplicate customer records accidentally appear in both training and validation sets. What issue is most likely?
questions.correctLabel: Data leakage between splits. Overlap between training and validation data can inflate apparent performance and hide generalization risk.
questions.extraHtml: Underfitting lowers both train and validation performance. Class imbalance can affect metrics, but split leakage directly explains suspicious validation gains.

questions.promptHtml: A fraud team lowers the decision threshold and catches more fraud but also blocks more legitimate purchases. Which metric tendency is most likely?

options.label: Precision and recall both always increase

questions.correctLabel: Recall increases while precision decreases. Lower thresholds classify more cases as positive, catching more true positives and more false positives.
questions.extraHtml: Precision-recall tradeoff is common in imbalanced risk models. You tune threshold based on business cost of false positives vs false negatives.

questions.promptHtml: A team compares deep learning options for image recognition and asks which architecture is traditionally strongest for spatial feature extraction in vision tasks. Which answer is best?
questions.correctLabel: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs). CNNs are designed to learn spatial hierarchies and local patterns in images.
questions.extraHtml: ARIMA is forecasting-oriented and K-means is unsupervised clustering. CNNs are the canonical baseline for many vision workloads.

questions.promptHtml: A security system must verify whether two face images belong to the same person before granting building access. Which service is most appropriate?
questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Face. It provides specialized face analysis and matching capabilities for face comparison scenarios.
questions.extraHtml: Translator and Search are not facial biometric services. Face-focused APIs are designed for this use case.

questions.promptHtml: An accounts payable workflow must extract vendor name, invoice number, totals, and line-item tables from PDFs. Which option best fits?

options.label: OCR read-only output with manual parsing

questions.correctLabel: Azure AI Document Intelligence. It extracts structured fields and tabular data from business documents.
questions.extraHtml: Read-only OCR provides text but not robust structured extraction. Speech services do not parse document layouts.

questions.promptHtml: A transport authority needs both road-scene classification (normal/congested) and per-vehicle counts by location. Which architecture is correct?

options.label: Combine image classification with object detection

questions.correctLabel: Combine image classification with object detection. Classification handles scene-level state, while detection provides object-level counts and locations.
questions.extraHtml: OCR and sentiment do not localize vehicles in images. Multi-task vision pipelines are common in traffic analytics.

questions.promptHtml: A retailer trains a custom image classifier with very few examples per class and poor lighting variety. Accuracy is unstable in production. What is the best explanation?

options.label: Insufficient representative labeled data for custom vision
Labels are optional in supervised classification

questions.correctLabel: Insufficient representative labeled data for custom vision. Model generalization depends on data quality, quantity, and diversity.
questions.extraHtml: Supervised classification requires labels, and lighting variation matters significantly for robust visual performance.

questions.promptHtml: A service desk bot must route requests based on user goals like ResetPassword and CancelOrder, not just extract keywords. Which NLP capability is most suitable?
questions.correctLabel: Conversational Language Understanding (intent recognition). Intent models are designed for goal-based routing in conversational systems.
questions.extraHtml: Key phrases identify topics but do not reliably infer actionable intent. OCR is not a conversational NLP router.

questions.promptHtml: A travel app must convert live spoken French into spoken English responses in near real time. Which capability is required?
questions.correctLabel: Speech translation. It combines speech recognition and translation for spoken multilingual interactions.
questions.extraHtml: Sentiment analysis does not perform bilingual speech conversion. Face detection is unrelated to audio translation.

questions.promptHtml: A quality team wants to detect sentiment shifts within one long review to separate product dissatisfaction from support dissatisfaction. Which feature is best?
questions.correctLabel: Sentence-level sentiment analysis. It reveals polarity at finer granularity than document-level scoring.
questions.extraHtml: Language detection identifies language, and key phrases identify themes. Neither alone gives sentence-by-sentence sentiment polarity.

questions.promptHtml: A compliance team must detect names, account IDs, and addresses in chat transcripts before archival. Which capability is most direct?

options.label: Named Entity Recognition and PII detection

questions.correctLabel: Named Entity Recognition and PII detection. These identify sensitive entities in text for masking and governance workflows.
questions.extraHtml: OCR is for document/image text extraction, while text-to-speech generates audio. Entity/PII analysis is the appropriate text-governance control.
